title: Lead, Manpower Development
description:
Lead, Manpower Development

about the company Our client is a leading global financial services provider committed to being a trusted partner for their customers' futures. With a strong presence in Singapore, they foster a culture where diversity is celebrated and professional growth is a priority. They offer a dynamic platform for individuals to make a real impact on the business while supporting long-term career ambitions.

about the job In this role, you will report to the Head of Recruitment and drive the strategic growth of the organization’s distribution team. Your primary focus is to develop and run innovative schemes that assist business partners in attracting and retaining talented financial professionals.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Creating strategic plans to grow manpower through new initiatives, incentive schemes, and engagement events.

  • Building platforms and marketing communications that enable partners to connect with potential talent effectively.

  • Monitoring industry trends and performing data research to improve recruitment and performance workflows.

  • Ensuring all recruitment policies and processes remain in line with the latest industry regulations and internal standards.

  • Guiding a team of managers to oversee various manpower projects and strengthening the overall recruitment value proposition.

  • Managing partnerships with external vendors and ensuring governance standards are met.

about the manager/team You will be guiding a dedicated team of Manpower Development Managers. You will work in a collaborative environment, partnering closely with Cluster Heads and Business Management teams to align recruitment efforts with overall business growth targets. The team is active, supportive, and follows through on creative problem-solving.

skills and experience required

  • A Bachelor’s Degree in any field of study.

  • At least 3 years of experience in recruitment or business development, preferably within the insurance or financial services sector.

  • Strong management capabilities with experience in supporting a team.

  • Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder engagement skills to work with business partners.

  • A mind for research with proficiency in data tools like MS Excel to develop reporting dashboards.

  • A proactive and adaptable person who thrives in an active environment.

  • Prior knowledge of industry-standard financial advisory certifications and regulatory requirements would be an advantage.

To apply online please use the 'apply' function, alternatively you may contact Hazel at +65 93884683. (EA: 94C3609/ R25158773)

postedCompany.description:
Randstad is a global talent leader with the vision to be the world’s most equitable and specialised talent company. As a partner for talent and through our four specialisations - Operational, Professional, Digital and Enterprise - we provide clients with the high-quality, diverse and agile workforces that they need to succeed in a talent scarce world. We help people secure meaningful roles, develop relevant skills and find purpose and belonging in their workplace. Through the value we create, we are committed to a better and more sustainable future for all.

In Singapore, we provide specialised professional recruitment and employer branding consultation services to companies in these industries and functions:

Accounting & Finance
Banking & Financial Services
Construction & Property
Corporate & Secretarial Support
Engineering
Human Resources
Information Technology
Legal
Life Science
Sales, Marketing and Communications
Supply Chain & Procurement

For more information, please visit www.randstad.com.sg.
postedCompany.name: RANDSTAD PTE. LIMITED
